title: Cane Cabinet
summary: A three-pane cane cabinet with modern straight lines and 3-shelf interior storage.
description: A three-pane cane cabinet with modern straight lines and 3-shelf interior storage. This cane cabinet is the perfect touch of modern mixed with trendy cane webbing, great for use as a wardrobe, pantry, linen cabinet and more!

materials.name: Open box weave cane webbing - 24" or 18" wide

steps.title: Attach boards to create top & bottom
steps.body: Glue and screw together boards to create the top and bottom: ¾” x 9 ½” x 36 ⅝”, ¾” x 4 ¾” x 36 ⅝”, and ¾” x 3 ½” x 36 ⅝”. Make 2.

steps.title: Attach boards to create side panels
steps.body: Glue and screw together boards to create side panels: ¾” x 5 ½” x 47 ½” and ¾” x 1 ½” x 47 ½”. Make 2.

steps.title: Attach side panels to backing
steps.body: Attach the side panels to the outermost side of backing using wood glue and 1 ¼” pocket hole screws.

steps.title: Attach the bottom
steps.body: Attach the bottom with the pocket holes facing up. Both the top and bottom pocket holes should be facing the same direction.

steps.title: Attach the top
steps.body: Attach the top with the pocket holes facing down. Both the top and bottom pocket holes should be facing the same direction.

steps.title: Assemble doors
steps.body: Put together doors using a 3” wood clamp at the joint and (2) 1 ¼” pocket-hole screws on either side of the 1 ½” x ¾” x 14 ⅜”. Your doors will be smaller than your opening at 3/32” gap around the door for an inset cabinet door.

steps.title: Drill concealed hinge holes
steps.body: Use your concealed hinge jig to make 2 hinge holes on either end of the cabinet door. The number of hinges is based on how heavy your door is (9-13lbs only needs 2 hinges).

steps.title: Attach legs
steps.body: Attach legs by screwing from the interior bottom of the cabinet down through the legs.

steps.title: Attach hinges
steps.body: Attach the hinge plate to the interior cabinet shell 2 3/16” from the front edge, directions will say 1 7/16”. You need to add the width of your door (¾”) to the 1 7/16”. Follow hinge installation guide instructions.

steps.title: Apply edgebanding
steps.body: Add oak edgebanding to one long edge of your 35 ⅛” x 15” x ¾” shelves. Attach shelves 15 ¾” from the top interior part of the cabinet to the top of the ¾” plywood shelf, using 1 ¼” pocket-hole screws.

steps.title: Attach cane webbing
steps.body: Use ¼” crown staples to attach your cane webbing at least a ½” overlay from the edge of the wood. Cut around the hole for your concealed hinges. Soak cane for 30 minutes in warm water before attaching for optimal usage. *Optional - Apply wood glue over staples and add a small trim piece if you wish to cover up the raw edge.

steps.title: Attach doors
steps.body: Clip in your hinges and attach doors to concealed hinges separately. With the 110-degree angle of the inset hinges, getting the hinges to clip into the hinge plate was difficult when already attached to the doors. Adjust hinges as necessary, there should be a 3/32” gap around the inset cabinet doors.

steps.title: Add interior door stopper
steps.body: Attach interior door stopper by using scrap wood, cut (2) 1 ½” x 3” pieces and glue 1 ½” inset from the edge. Add this to the top and bottom to stop the doors from over extending into the cabinet.

steps.title: Add safety braces
steps.body: For safety, please brace the cabinet to the wall using anti-tip hardware for use around children.
